content.problemStatement:
There are NNN cities (numbered from 111 to NNN) and MMM bidirectional roads. The ithi^{th}ith of these roads connects the cities AiA_iAi‚Äã and BiB_iBi‚Äã and has a length of DiD_iDi‚Äã Km.

There are only KKK cities that have a hospital for corona testing. The cost of corona testing may be different in different cities. For each city, we want to know the minimum cost that needs to be spent to test a corona sample. If city you are in contains a hospital and you choose to test in that hospital itself, then the cost incurred is the cost of corona test in that hospital. Otherwise, if you choose to test in a hospital in another city, then an additional transportation cost is also incurred (Assuming Rs.1 per Km).

So from a city iii, if you choose to send a sample to city jjj, then the total cost incurred will be Cj+D(i,j)C_j + D(i,j)Cj‚Äã+D(i,j). Here CjC_jCj‚Äã denotes the cost of corona testing in a hospital situated in city jjj and D(i,j)D(i,j)D(i,j) denotes the minimum distance between city iii and jjj via some road network.

Output the minimum money that should be spent by a person in each of the NNN cities for testing a corona sample.

It is guaranteed that there is a path from every city to every other city. Also, there is at most one road between any pair of cities (i.e. no multiple edges). There are no roads from one city to itself (i.e. no self-loops).

**Note:** Since the inputs and outputs are large, prefer using fast input-output methods.


Input Format

The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
Each testcase contains K+M+1K + M + 1K+M+1 lines of input.
The first line of each test case contains three space-separated integers N,M,KN, M, KN,M,K.
KKK  lines follow. For each valid iii, the ithi^{th}ith of these lines contains two space-separated integers xi,Cix_i, C_ixi‚Äã,Ci‚Äã which denotes that there is a hospital in the xithx_i^{th}xith‚Äã city and the cost of corona testing in that hospital is CiC_iCi‚Äã. There is at most one hospital in a city i.e. all xix_ixi‚Äã-s are distinct.
Next MMM lines follow. For each valid iii, the ithi^{th}ith of these lines contains three space-separated integers Ai,Bi,DiA_i, B_i, D_iAi‚Äã,Bi‚Äã,Di‚Äã, which denotes that there is a bidirectional road between City AiA_iAi‚Äã and City BiB_iBi‚Äã of length DiD_iDi‚Äã Km.


Output Format
For each test case, print a single line containing NNN space-separated integers, where the ithi^{th}ith integer denotes the minimum cost of corona testing if you are in the ithi^{th}ith city.
constraints: 1‚â§xi,Ai,Bi‚â§N1 \leq x_i, A_i, B_i \leq N1‚â§xi‚Äã,Ai‚Äã,Bi‚Äã‚â§N for each valid iii
Sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2‚ãÖ1052 \cdot 10^52‚ãÖ105
Sum of MMM over all test cases does not exceed 4‚ãÖ1054 \cdot 10^54‚ãÖ105
All xix_ixi‚Äã-s in a testcase are distinct.
It is guaranteed that there is a path from every city to every other city.
It is guaranteed that there are no self loops and multiple edges.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: The hospitals are situated at cities 111 and 222 with their costs being 555 and 10 respectively. For city 111 it is optimal to test the sample in its own hospital bearing a total cost of 555. For city 222 it is again optimal to test the sample in its own hospital bearing a total cost of 101010. For city 333 it is optimal to test the sample in the hospital at city 222 which incurs the traveling cost of 999 and hospital cost of 101010, so the total cost is 10+9=1910+9=1910+9=19.
Test case 222: For city 222, it is optimal to test the sample in hospital at city 111 which incurs a traveling cost of 111 and hospital cost of 555, so the total cost is 1+5=61+5=61+5=6.
